channel.title: EMBER_DECK
channel.generator: Webpage HTML Export plugin for Obsidian

item.title: CHARACTER CREATION
item.description:
Start with a concept: who is this character, and what do they do in the world? Pick an Oath: this sets your core Prisms (MIGHT, GUILE, INSIGHT) and starting deck identity.
Build your starting deck: use the listed Oath deck, then apply any allowed starter options, shards, or oath-specific choices. (OATH_OF_THE_PYRE OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT OATH_OF_THE_MISTWALKER)
Choose your CHARGE.
Optional: add personality using Quirks from the TALK_THE_TALK for inspiration, especially if you want extra flavour beyond the mechanics. Write a short backstory for the GM, in collaboration with the other players, explaining how you're bonded, unless it is pre-specified in the hook. Oprtional: If you're playing a One-shot, or a shorter campaign, it is likely you will be using a 3-2-1 Prism distribution instead of the standard 2-1-0 +1.
OATH_OF_THE_PYRE
Passive: MEND MIGHT VITALS at combat end.
CHARGE: Sacrifice up to MIGHT VITALS, gain as much BRACE, and 2 X MOMENTUM
Prism Scores: 2 GUILE, 1 INSIGHT, 3 MIGHT.
OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT
Passive: Your IGNITE effects do not decay.
CHARGE: IGNITE INSIGHT Distribute the stacks as you wish. Already IGNITEd enemies take their total IGNITE stacks as DMG immediately (Does not remove stacks).
Prism Scores: 1 GUILE, 3 INSIGHT, 2 MIGHT.
OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT
Passive: Leftover GUARD is halved at turn end, instead of vanishing completely.
CHARGE: STRIKE = Missing VITALS
Prism Scores: 2 GUILE, 3 INSIGHT, 1 MIGHT.
OATH_OF_THE_MISTWALKER
Passive: Discard the first negative STATUS drawn (by You) each turn without triggering its effects.
CHARGE: Reduce the cost of all cards currently dealt to 1 this turn.
Prism Scores: 3 GUILE, 2 INSIGHT, 1 MIGHT.
OATH_OF_THE_PYRE
Passive: At the end of each combat round, gain +1 MOMENTUM.
CHARGE: STRIKE with MOMENTUM multiplier (DMG = STRIKE X MOMENTUM, instead of STRIKE + MOMENTUM).
Prism Scores: 2 GUILE, 1 INSIGHT, 3 MIGHT.
OATH_OF_THE_MISTWALKER
Passive: Dealing unblocked HP damage grants +1 CHARGE.
CHARGE: Stun an enemy. (Pause their Intent)
Prism Scores: 3 GUILE, 1 INSIGHT, 2 MIGHT.

item.title: index
item.description:
By clicking or typing in the link to this site you have agreed to not share any content, ideas, or concepts you find here without direct permission from our developers *Ember Deck is a project still in early development, so this site may update and look unrecognisable after the fact!Said plainly, Ember Deck is a Dieselpunk TTRPG Deckbuilder made for (ideally) 3-4 Player groups, but it should still scale relatively well until about 5-6 people. It was an idea heavily inspired by gems such as "Slay The Spire", "Hearthstone", "Slice and Dice", and "Morimens". The gameplay features tight resource management in a low-math, high-keyword environment, giving a couple of anchor points to make your own character and their deck unique and powerful, whereas the Setting provides plenty of opportunity for "angsty" or "against all odds" roleplay in a grimdark world, where magic is ubiquitous, but hope is scant.Once, there was HELL. Every day, souls passed in and out, getting incinerated and sent to the Hall of Echoes, pure. One day an apocalyptic event happened. Because of that, HELL is working overtime and is still decades behind. Because of that, Souls get clogged up in the in-between, and that radiation seeps into the living. Until finally, their bodies can't take it anymore, and burn up from the inside.*The LORE dropdown is a good place to start for anyone curious about the world and the gritty reality found therein, ex.: VIBE, LORE>IN-WORLD, HEARTHS AND EMBERS. For the Rules Lawyers: here's the short end of it, as the detailed rulings fall under their own dropdowns, labelled BS, DS, CARDS, or CHARACTER.Once a concept of who you'd like to play as forms in your mind, the preferable place to go is the CHARACTER>OATHS Section, where you'll find all three major Oaths (which function like classes, and determine your starting Attributes - Prisms), these in turn determine your Starting Deck.
All Oaths start with 10 BACKBONE cards making up their deck, but the keywords may vary based on chosen Oath and even subtle differences between the same Oath's decks are possible at times.
Now, having a concept, embodied by an Oath, and a Deck, you select your CHARGE, and your Passives (Each Oath offers a different selection). These are the anchor points mentioned before - they'll define your playstyle heavily. Once that's in order, and your friends have done the same, you're set! (Don't forget to send at least 4 sentences worth of a backstory to your GM, though.)As with most RPGs, the weight of this point lands mostly on the GM, however some things to keep in mind as a Player skipping through sections: Checks are made with d6's, many of them at times (up to 8-ish). Miniatures are welcome but leave the rulers at home - combat doesn't use movement. Agree on a vision you and the other players have on your bond, how you met and connected, it will save the GM a couple of grey hairs. Enjoy! Changed Keyword function and Naming:
EMPOWER -> MOMENTUM.
EMPOWER was Changed to provide a readily accessible pool instead of a small trickle.
BRACE was updated to mirror this change.
Focus -> EMPOWER.
Ward now scales with Insight and prevents enemy keywords as well as statuses.
Delay was Removed.
Added CHARACTER/IMAGES sections across the vault to provide visuals.
CASE STUDY was removed for cleanup Certain notes have received related images inside the notes
CINDERS, and, HEARTHS AND EMBERS were moved to the FRACTIONS dropdown
The SYNC-AXIS is now more firmly tied to combat through the updated EMPOWERand BRACE keywords
CHARGE note was corrected, showing an improved formula for post-burst draw calculation
INVENTORY section was brushed up, adding needed specifications and removing unnecessary repetitions. Allowed Curios to have limited Mechanical value.
Clarified PIERCE's scaling
Removed false keyword scalings from GUILE, INSIGHT, and MIGHT
Clarified SHARDS in-combat effects, and removed Delay from the list.
Added the EXPLORATION section
Added Several Notes explaining the rules of EXPLORATION (NODE - RESTNODE - PATHNODE - UNKNOWNNODE - UNSTABLENODE - ENCOUNTER, and REFERENCE IMAGE)
Expanded the TURNS page with an infographic, which can also be found in ENEMIES>RULINGS
Added HEARTH SHEET as well as EMBER SHEET
Compiled existing notes into a single file INFECTION
Streamlined LIGHTNING
Made the HOLLOW effect more inconvenient Changed the Oath of the Crucible to Oath of the Pyre for easier differentiation and recognition from Conduit
Introduced Party-influenced STATUS cards added when taking DMG at 0 VITALS and reshuffling the shared deck during prolonged combats
Added the DECK note for clarifications of card handling
Expanded on the Tools and Curios section within INVENTORY
Altered MISTWALKER PASSIVES to DISCARDthe first status instead of resisting an enemy intent for wider versatility
Created Intent Loops for Firebomber.canvas and Cultist.canvas and deleted Cinder Goliath.png
Also added a short guide to reading Intent loops in the RULINGS section
Also in the RULINGS section, introduced a potentially volatile mechanic of Front and Back row enemies. To be tested.
Clarified interactions between MOMENTUM and AREA and LIGHTNING
Redefined OATH_OF_THE_PYRE CHARACTER/OATHS/PYRE/CHARGES to not rely on MOMENTUM so heavily, changing one entirely to propose a new playstyle
Added a third option for handling hitting 0 Vitals.
Changed SHARDS in-combat complexity Added several new Enemies, and expanded upon the existing ones
Changed Reshuffle rules: The Shared DECK now keeps the reshuffle count between combats and resets at rest sites only.
WARD was removed. Rest sites are now explicitly Reusable.
NODE - UNKNOWN now has a roll table of effects.
GUARD's effects can synergise with certain Passives and Trinkets
ECHO was removed.
Added suggested Encounter Templates to the RULINGS tab.
Added a survivability QoL for Enemies in the form of Innate Stacking GUARD
IGNITE has been refreshed to keep it interesting and Viable
Changed BLEED to scale with MIGHT to keep relative balance with each Prism now having 3 Primary keywords each.
Updated BLEED to be more interactive, bringing it up to speed with the previously tweaked IGNITE
Prepared new Ember and Hearth Sheets for the next Playtest down the line. They can be found in the EMBER SHEET and HEARTH SHEET notes.
Added a new STATUS - INFECTION which balances out the improved VAMPIRIC and High-MEND parties.
Updated LEVEL_UP to include proper Node names as well as include the conditional for EXHAUST retrieval, and clearing up Adding new cards
EXHAUST has been explained in more detail.
Changed the odds for NODE - UNKNOWN so it can no longer trigger an NPC/Lore event, and instead is empty more often
Added newly designed, visually polished improvements for the EMBER SHEET and HEARTH SHEET. Moved the info about Prisms to the Character dropdown. Updated Prisms section with accurate scaling keywords.
Temporarily removed MERCILESS.
Improved upon the design and numbers on some Enemies, also added the Trench Horror (Hunter).canvas, as well as a dropdown with Encounter Templates, ranging from Basic through Difficult all the way to Hard.
Made TREMBLING more dangerous by increasing its Cost to play.
Made MIST less threatening by delaying its activation.
Changed Merciless to apply to ANY targettable card (Strike, Mend, Bleed, Ignite), but also to scale with the player's highest Prism, lessening MIGHT's dominance.
Tweaked starting Oath decks, balancing the Empower/Mend pivot for OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT, and tossing in a starting MOMENTUM for the OATH_OF_THE_PYRE, to allow easier tasting of the other (Non- STRIKE/GUARD) keywords.
Removed the Oath-Only-new card restriction from the LEVEL_UP options
Split up the pages for TOOLS, CURIOS, and ARTEFACTS from the INVENTORY section.
Introduced a pilot mechanic called Madness, which is a result of exploring the MIST without proper equipment. Stabilised the rulings regarding item bonuses and applications so players and GMs alike can make more informed decisions about their equipment.
Added strictness to CURIOS rulings so they don't overlap as much with ARTEFACTS and instead fill their own role, one card at a time.
More examples of CURIOS and ARTEFACTS effects.
Clarified the wording on BS ROLLS (CHECKS).
Moved around help text on the LEVEL_UP tab.
Removed the EXHAUST mention from SHARDS as to avoid repetition.
Removed the "Affects ALL allies" text from AREA, as the players' stats and buffs/debuffs are shared anyway.
Improved MERCILESS scaling to reward target picking.
Improved PIERCE by removing its scaling.
Elaborated on the EXHAUST impact on deckbuilding.
Rephrased the INJURY wording to match the keywords used throughout the system.
Nerfed the OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT passive that allowed the rest of the party to ignore their INSIGHT scores completely.
Narrowed down OATH_OF_THE_MISTWALKER passive that discarded drawn STATUS cards.
Specified the N in OATH_OF_THE_PYRE Burst page meant Player count, to match the system wording.
Specified the trigger condition for PYRE PASSIVES.
Big CHARGE Rework, removing the ENERGY cost, introducing infinite Charge accumulation, and bonuses tied to it, as well as gentler next-turn-draw-penalty rules. Uploaded Images to the Character Section, to help visualise various playable archetypes.
Added ominous three dots "..." in the DECK page.
Added a rule of thumb to the SYNC-AXIS page.
Removed the outdated combat tracker from the TURNS page.
Added a description to the WAR page.
Separated pages for HEROIC-SOCIETY, MAGIC-SOCIETY, DIESEL-SOCIETY, CLASSICAL-SOCIETY and a GM_Guide for deepening them. Names TBD.
Added the REDACTED tag to any pages in need of large edits.
Added NODE - PORTAL, NODE - DOOR, and NODE - REWARD as they appeared on REFERENCE IMAGE but were not present in the rules.
Created the CHARACTER CREATION page. It is empty.
Polished and released a beta for the TALK_THE_TALK. Introduced NPC Quirks in a specialised Table.
Conceptualised and added the Gotcha minigame.
Introduced a new faction - NOMAD-SOCIETY.
Added a variation of the NODE - REWARD, NODE - RITUAL. Attempted to make combat more dynamic by introducing a slightly altered layout, and set phases to de-complicate tracking.
Formalised combat rewards in the SHARDS page.
Altered Backbone Purist as an archetype, bringing it to speed with the other styles of play. BACKBONE
Removed the ECONOMICS and CULTURE pages as they were left empty long enough for me to develop Societies with their own takes on these issues, see NOMAD-SOCIETY, MAGIC-SOCIETY, HEROIC-SOCIETY, DIESEL-SOCIETY, CLASSICAL-SOCIETY for more.
Updated the index page itself, adding further rules briefing. Briefly lost my mind as the newest deployments stopped showing patch notes after 0.7.3
Cleaned up and split the RULINGS section into smaller, easier to digest chunks. See REINFORCEMENTS, LOOPS.
Added separate pages for each major Turned type, see HUSKS, HOLLOW, PHENOMENA. The CARDS dropdown was separated from CHARACTER as it also contains info about non-player cards.
Added symbolic graphics to STATUS cards (individually)
Added symbolic graphics to MIGHT, GUILE, and INSIGHT pages, alongside some brief flavour text.
Cleaned up the EROSION page and differentiated a COLLAPSE page from it.
Switched the naming of BURST to CHARGE. Inconsistencies will be hunted down but may take a while due to the 120 potential suspect-files.
Buffed PIERCE giving it more utility after the combat tracking changes
Added a prototype enemy card in the RULINGS page to better illustrate its function.
Renamed the IMAGES section in the LORE>WORLD>PHYSICAL PILLAR>LOCATIONS to CITIES Added reference images for all the Societies reworked in the last update.
Actually wrote something for the CHARACTER CREATION page Added new enemies:
Mourner.canvas
Clock.canvas
Stalker.canvas
Feeder.canvas
Tethers.canvas
Tweaked the descriptions on VIBE, and HELL
Finally added something to FAITHS
Reinvented ARTEFACTS, giving them stronger effects but tying Banes to the Boons.
Added the promised MADNESS status, and updated the associated NODE - UNKNOWN source of acquisition.
Formalised movement rules for EXPLORATION
Added a new NODE - PREDATOR.
Added more difficult variants to the standard BS ROLLS (CHECKS)
Moved CURSED to Tertiary keywords Tidied up the Status cards layouts for easier reading
Touched upon CHARGES, CHARGES, and CHARGES, adding 2 extra options per. Added 2 passives options per Oath, and named the appropriate notes more clearly Added images to many of the core and Lore sections Created a Lore In-World folder for both Personal and REDACTED accounts of events. Added content therein Fixed an issue where images would not show up properly in-browser
Added a A Guide to Doctrines concept page. In its folder (BS>Doctrines) you will also find the selection for all Society-themed Doctrines, a brand new mechanic that ties to the world and cities of Ember Deck
Added a conditional to BLEED, giving it a more defined Niche.
Introduced FLEEING.
Removed UNIQUE cards page as it rarely saw any use to justify its existence. Added Tags to all pages within the Vault, Categorising them as either: BASE-GAME Oaths Energy Turns Enemy Loops Deck(s) Shards Vitals Prisms Rolls (checks) Keywords* Talk The Talk Deck Tinkering Nodes Predator Portal Path Door Rest Unstable Encounter EXPANSIONS IDENTITIES Oath Passives Oath Actives Charge Backbone Decay Gotcha Sync-Axis SOCIETIES The recently introduced Doctrines Node - Ritual Fleeing HQ INVENTORIES Tools, Curios, Artefacts While Keywords are an essential part of the Base Game, they were getting in the way of Tag-based search, and as such they were subsequently tagged KEYWORD instead Minor wording corrections and spelling fixes across the vault.
The HQ note is present but is also a mess. Rebalanced and mostly buffed certain enemies to better match the expected handling of N+1 Intents per turn. Removed repeating enemy archetypes like the Saint and the Bulwark. Added enemy categories: Horrors and Humans (the stats can be mixed and matched regardless of these categories but it can help with clarity and atmosphere). Removed Encounter presets as they proved unreliable with just +/- 1 party member.
Added the Machinegunner.canvas, Goon.canvas enemy types Removed Images from the Playerside and Enemyside DS rules.
Added further clarification for the RETAIN keyword
Introduced a cap for active SHARDS in the shared deck due to pollution problems revealed in the recent Playtest.
Also added a "End of Combat" section within the TURNS one.
NODE - PREDATOR will now continue to chase until it catches the party, or pursuit becomes impossible.
Added example character builds in the CHARACTER CREATION page.

item.title: CONDUIT PASSIVES
item.description:
Leftover GUARD is halved at turn end, instead of vanishing completely.
Your IGNITE effects do not decay.
Whenever two cards with matching keywords are played consecutively, the effect triggers once more, using Your raw INSIGHT score for Value.
Overhealing from any source grants the healer CHARGE equal to the amount overhealed.
First card played each turn costs -1 ENERGY.
EMPOWER INSIGHT the first card played each turn.

item.title: PYRE PASSIVES
item.description:
MEND MIGHT VITALS at combat end.
If Your STRIKE OR BLEED kills an enemy, all excess STRIKE damage OR BLEED stacks is/are applied the next (left to right) target.
Discarding for CHARGE charges grants You +3 instead of +2 charges per Card's ENERGY cost.
Whenever the Hearth takes VITALS damage, You gain +1 CHARGE Charge (Per Activation, not per DMG).
At the end of each combat round, gain +1 MOMENTUM.
Every time You kill an enemy in combat, gain a stacking +1 BLEED value this combat.

item.title: NODE - PREDATOR
item.description:
Predator Encounters have their Domains, which are always an area of 7 hexes, but the shape of them is entirely map-dependent (think of water filling a container). The Domain outline, and the Predator, is drawn at the start of the exploration, for transparency (and lack of a better method of map presentation as of right now).Predator Encounters will activate once the players step on any of their Domain Nodes, and will follow them until successful, or cut off by a NODE - UNSTABLE.
Predator Encounters move 1 space after the group finishes moving, and 2 spaces after a NODE - REST, NODE - RITUAL, or NODE - REWARD.
If a NODE - PREDATOR ends its move on a Node adjacent to the group, Combat is initiated.

item.title: VITALS
item.description:
This is the Sum of All Players' Prisms + N (no. Players) X 2 When Vitals hits 0, the deck's top card is flipped, and its owner must decide: Shatter: Immediately remove 1 of their CUSTOMS of choice from the deck. Culling: The player reduces one Prism score by 1 (Post-combat).
Trauma: Add a random, persistent STATUS card from this selection:
WOUND
PANIC
DOUBT
INJURY
TREMBLING
These cards stay in the deck unless specifically removed using SHARDS. The owner is required to take the card they "caused" whenever it appears during play, ignoring the standard STATUS card dealing rules.
Regardless of their decision, N x STATUS cards are shuffled into the shared deck. These cards are based on the Party's Oath choices:
OATH_OF_THE_MISTWALKER causes a MIST
OATH_OF_THE_PYRE cases an EROSION
OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT causes a HOLLOW
Each subsequent hit also shuffles in a stacking amount of ASH cards (for a 3-player group this would look like so);
1st Hit: 1x MIST, 1x EROSION, 1x HOLLOW.
2nd Hit: 1x MIST, 1x EROSION, 1x HOLLOW + 1x ASH.
3rd Hit: 1x MIST, 1x EROSION, 1x HOLLOW. + 2x ASH.
If the topdeck revealed card is a STATUS one itself, the player with the most CHARGE, or Highest Prism score sum is the decisionmaker. Max Vitals are recalculated at combat end only.
When hit at 0 Vitals, it is also an option to Sacrifice oneself. The player's character dies, but, they release their CHARGE immediately, with no extra costs nor limitations. Their Cards are then taken ot of the deck, and replaced with HOLLOW.
Death can also occur when an Ember's Prism scores all hit 0. The Ember's cards are then still removed from the deck, but are not replaced with any STATUS cards.

item.title: TURNS
item.description:
Fights are a constant back-and-forth between the Hearth-linked Embers and their opponents, following a simple flow: Setup. - Start-of-encounter effects. - Player passives and Artefacts trigger. - Enemy passives can trigger here. - Initial intents are revealed. Player turn. - Cards are dealt (N x 3). - Energy is regained (N x 2). - Players play and resolve their cards in order. - The rest are discarded, if possible. Enemy turn. - Intents are realized left to right, starting at the second line. Then left to right on the first line. End of round. - Bleed/Ignite tick.
- Player GUARDis removed - Reinforcements arrive. - New intents are shown. After round 1, the game becomes: Player turn → Enemy turn → End of round Cleaning the Deck.
Remove any non-Trauma (see VITALS) STATUS cards (with the exception of HOLLOW). This includes UNPLAYABLE cards with RETAIN from the players' hands. Do not reorder or shuffle the remaining cards in the process. Clearing the Hearth Sheet.
Remove any Negative Stacks such as BLEED, or IGNITE.
Positive Stacks, such as MOMENTUM, BRACE, or COUNTER are Halved, then kept for the next encounter.
Receive SHARDS as per their formula
Only up to 2xN SHARDS can be inside the deck at once. The rest are simply placed aside for the next LEVEL_UP or,
If any SHARDS were spent during combat, replenish them up to their cap.

item.title: RETAIN
item.description: Card stays in hand if unplayed. It also Persists in hand across combats. Reminder: The hand limit is 5 cards

item.title: DECK
item.description:
Each turn, players draw cards using the N x 3 formula, from the shared deck, and take their own cards into their Hands. STATUS cards are dealt out evenly as described.
The Hand limit is 5 cards, unless overruled by an Artefact or narrative effects. If you cannot hold any more cards, and they have already been drawn, they are moved to the discard pile immediately and generate no CHARGE charges while doing so.
Once the shared deck runs dry, a Reshuffle is necessary, however, each Reshuffle also adds N status cards, akin to the 0 VITALS situation (also stacking, meaning the third reshuffle will pollute the shared deck with 3xN cards). The Reshuffle counter only resets through a rest, so drawing out one encounter will make you suffer in the next one...
There is a cap of 2xN SHARDS that are "active" in the shared deck at any time, to prevent deck pollution and overspending what is essentially XP points.

item.title: SHARDS
item.description:
Shards are the universal currency of the Hearth. They act as both long-term progression and can be crushed as mid-combat consumables: Cost: 0 ENERGY.
Effect: grant +1 ENERGY AND +1 DRAW Crushed Shards are gone forever and cannot be used for progression. MIGHT = enemies defeated. 1 enemy kill = 1 Shard/10 HP the enemy had. INSIGHT = full intent loops realized (understanding the enemy). 1 FULL intent Loop realised = 3 Shard. GUILE = fewer turns taken / efficiency. Baseline Turns (=2x Enemy count - N (Player count)). Baseline Turns - Turns Taken = 2 Shards each. Thus the rewards push a “pick 2 of 3” shape: get out of there fast, watch and learn, You can’t get both every fight.
There is a cap of 2xN SHARDS that are "active" in the shared deck at any time, to prevent deck pollution and overspending what is essentially XP points.

item.title: SYNC-AXIS
item.description:
The relationship between the Embers dictates their combat performance and playstyle. It should mainly be the Players that decide when the marker moves across the Axis, as they roleplay among themselves. The scale ranges from -3 to +3, and is tied to ENERGY and DMG variables. GM rule of thumb:
Always judge the SYNC-AXIS by the weakest chain/bond.

ADD 2x TREMBLING
BLEED (5)KILL CIt'll be a hydraesque enemy, that shuffles each head's intent to the right, and, when a head is killed, it loses both its unique intent (ex. Guard), AND a whole column And I know what you're thinking - once they sniff this out they'll kill the Strike and Bleed "heads" and stall the fight And here's the genius of Trembling. Trembling makes you take DMG per Energy spent so the enemy can still damage you without any actual damage intents, BECAUSE YOU STILL NEED TO KILL IT SOMEHOW And if they focus the dmg heads they will allow significant amounts of both trembling and Mist to pollute the deck (mist also self replicates, so it may happen that it will just be ALL you draw at some point)

item.title: INJURY
item.description:
Cost: 2 Energy. RETAINEXHAUST.
While in hand, your CHARGE is UNPLAYABLE.

item.title: MADNESS
item.description:
UNPLAYABLE RETAIN
When drawn, place the card from your hand onto your EMBER SHEET. Your Passive is locked while it remains there. This card will stay on your sheet until the next reshuffle, or combat end, whichever comes first.
Madness can be DISCARDed / EXHAUSTed by other cards/CHARGEs/effects.

item.title: STATUS
item.description:
STATUS cards are Negative effects applied by Enemies, Events, Reshuffles, CURSED, or through losing VITALS. Unless noted otherwise (by having their ENERGY cost listed), these are UNPLAYABLE, and disappear at the end of combat (with the exception of HOLLOW, and statuses added via VITALS loss).
STATUS cards are ownerless, and so are dealt out randomly from left to right starting from players with the least amount of STATUS cards in hand.

item.title: WOUND
item.description:
UNPLAYABLE
When drawn, take 2 PIERCE DMG.

item.title: TREMBLING
item.description:
Cost: 2 Energy. EXHAUST.
While in hand, playing another card deals Self DMG = ENERGY cost of the card played.

item.title: PANIC
item.description:
RETAIN
Cost: 1 Energy. When drawn, DISCARD another card from your hand. It does not contribute to CHARGE Gauge when discarded this way.

item.title: MIST
item.description: RETAIN UNPLAYABLE When drawn, shuffle a MIST into the Discard Pile.

item.title: INFECTION
item.description:
UNPLAYABLE.
While in hand all Vitals Restoring effects (MEND, VAMPIRIC) deal DMG instead.
*(This does mean you can damage enemies using your MEND cards, and take unavoidable DMG from your VAMPIRIC cards)

item.title: HOLLOW
item.description: Cost: 1 Energy. No effect. Persists between combats. Dissipates on its own during a LEVEL_UP/NODE - REST

item.title: EROSION
item.description: Cost: 1 Energy. When drawn, take 1 DMG. DRAW 1 card when played.

item.title: DOUBT
item.description:
RETAIN UNPLAYABLE
At the end of turn, lose 1 CHARGE charge.

item.title: ASH
item.description:
RETAIN
Cost: 2 Energy. EXHAUST when played.

item.title: UNPLAYABLE
item.description: Cards with this keyword cannot be played, only Discarded or Exhausted by the effects of other cards, CHARGE, or other means.

item.title: EXHAUST
item.description:
Cards with this keyword are removed from the deck after being played. They can be restored at a NODE - REST at the cost of 1 SHARDS. Since they remove a card from play, they count against the Minimum Deck limit, as if being removed for the counting.
Reminder: Cannot have less than 4 BACKBONE cards in your deck, meaning that 6/10 (for example) cards in your deck can be Exhaustible, if you wish so.

item.title: DRAW
item.description: Draw 1 card(s).

item.title: DISCARD
item.description: Discard 1 card(s). Cards discarded this way give CHARGE charges equal to their cost (as if they were played), to their Owner.

item.title: CURSED
item.description: Adding a CURSED Keyword to a card makes it add a random STATUS card into the shared deck when played, But, it reduces the card's overall cost by 2 ENERGY (effectively -1 reduction as CURSEDare still technically keywords and so they increase a card's cost by 1).

item.title: VAMPIRIC
item.description: Restore VITALS equal to 1/2 Value when hitting HP. Stacks.

item.title: PIERCE
item.description: Ignores Block. Allows targeting of backrow (2nd line) enemies even if they're behind an enemy.

item.title: MERCILESS
item.description: If target has no Block, add 2xValue to the card's total Value. Scales with Your highest Prism.

item.title: LIGHTNING
item.description: Hits two targets: One with the lowest and one with the highest HP. In case of only one target available hits it twice.

item.title: AREA
item.description: Affects ALL enemies.

item.title: STRIKE
item.description: Deal Value DMG. Scales with MIGHT

item.title: MOMENTUM
item.description:
Pool of Value. When dealing DMG with STRIKE you may expend any amount of stacks from the pool and add it to the STRIKE dealt. Pool decreases when HP damage is taken.
Depending on the SYNC-AXIS; Harmony - halve, rounding up. Discord - remove all stacks.
Scales with MIGHT
Momentum damage is never duplicated, multiplied, or applied to more than one enemy. In the case or AREA being present on the card triggering MOMENTUM, pick a single target to receive the extra damage. In the case of LIGHTNING, the Higher HP target takes the bonus.

item.title: MEND
item.description:
Restores Value Vitality. Can remove BLEED stacks before they deal damage.
Scales with INSIGHT

item.title: IGNITE
item.description:
Deal 1 DMG at the end of the enemy's turn, then decrease stacks by 1. If Stacks reach 9, remove all stacks and deal 9 DMG immediately. Players may choose to Vent their Ignite stacks, taking DMG = no. Stacks cleared. This counts as DMG, interacting with VITALS and MOMENTUM/BRACE rules.
Scales with INSIGHT

item.title: GUARD
item.description: Grants Value Block - stopping incoming DMG. Scales with GUILE

item.title: EMPOWER
item.description: Adds Value to the numeric effect of the next card played. Scales with INSIGHT

item.title: COUNTER
item.description: Grants Value stacks. Deals Value DMG back when hit; halve stacks (round up) after each trigger. Scales with GUILE

item.title: BRACE
item.description:
Pool of Value. When gaining GUARD you may expend any amount of stacks from the pool and add it to the total GUARD received. Pool decreases when HP damage is taken.
Depending on the SYNC-AXIS; Harmony - halve, rounding up. Discord - remove all stacks.
Scales with GUILE

item.title: BLEED
item.description:
Deal Value DMG at the end of enemy's turn, bypassing GUARD. If applied to a wounded enemy, double the BLEED value.
Can be prevented by Healing (even at max HP/Vitals.) The would-be-healed amount is removed from current BLEED stacks before restoring HP/VITALS
Scales with MIGHT

item.title: PHENOMENA
item.description: Phenomena are individual Turned that break the norm, their Collapse either more sudden or powerful, or both. As such, their forms are more erratic and visibly distinct, causing all the more terror when witnessing such an abnormality. While their corporeal forms may resemble that of HUSKS or HOLLOW, their heads are almost always replaced with a supernatural phenomenon, hinting at the unusual nature of these beings.

item.title: HUSKS
item.description: These are the usual effects of EROSION and soon-to-follow Collapse. They are the most human, which makes them all the more horrifying to encounter. Often appearing in groups, as one person's Collapse is likely to cause a chain reaction within a crowd.

item.title: HOLLOWS
item.description: These creatures emerge from the carcasses of HUSKS once they grow cold. They are impossible shadowy silhouettes that move and act in unusual ways, already detached from any humanity. They may take on appearance of people, and even confine their forms with clothing, but make no mistake, these are not attempts to fit in society, but merely conceal themselves for the hunt.

item.title: FLEEING
item.description:
If you're feeling outnumbered, outmatched, or both, your Hearth may always attempt to Flee a combat encounter. To do so, simply roll 2d6, subtract the number of remaining enemies, and compare the resulting number to the amount of players.Say there's 2x Husk.canvas, and 3x Shrieker.canvas pinning you down, your VITALS are going into single-digit territory, and your comrades decide you should all Flee. You roll 2d6. The result is a perfectly balanced 6 and a 1, giving a total of 7. There are 5 enemies total, so your roll of 7 becomes a 2. Since there are 4 of you, the attempt is failed, and you must progress with your turn as normal. You may re-attempt to Flee after either:
Your VITALS amount has changed (probably for the worse). OR Enemy numbers have changed (be it from murder or reinforcements).

item.title: GOTCHA
item.description: It is essentially a hypothetical combat boast / contest over whether you can “outplay” another player’s line. When declaring a "Gotcha" the initiator is basically saying "If I played my hand against yours, I could damage You, without taking a hit myself". Uses actual CHARGE charges as chips. The point is to give excess charges a place to matter and to let ownership of charges shift between players in a dramatic way. The loser’s charges are transferred through to the winner. If the pot grows, enemies may flee: Flee chance = number of chips in the pot as a percentage, so 30 chips = 30% flee chance, per turn, not per enemy. Free opt-out if the "defender" has fewer cards than the initiator. That gives the minigame a clean threshold and makes hand size matter in a readable way. It also prevents the whole thing from becoming a mandatory duel every time someone says “Gotcha.” There is also a One-Gotcha-Per-Player-Per-Exploration suggestion to avoid just that.Starting bet low enough to matter but not so high that it is spammed. A good range: Start at 5 charges for a basic Gotcha. Start at 10 charges for a more serious challenge. Raise in increments of 5.   That keeps the whole thing readable and ensures the minigame is about confidence and momentum, not just who has the largest stockpile. One check per transfer (if the transfer is >10 charges), not one per 10 charges. So: when charges transfer, the winner makes one check total, the size of the transfer may modify that check, but it does not create multiple separate checks. Having two sides and letting the rest of the table pick sides turns Gotcha into a spectator bet instead of a private duel. One player challenges another. Other players can join either side. They contribute charges to the pool. The winner takes the pot, and the group gets the fun of social momentum around the exchange.

item.title: ENERGY
item.description:
Used to play cards. The cost of a card equals the number of Keywords on it. A Party's ENERGY Max is calculated as follows: N (no. Players) X 2. The Hearth replenishes all its ENERGY at every turn start unless noted otherwise.
The Maximum ENERGY available can also be influenced by the SYNC-AXIS.

item.title: RULINGS
item.description:
Enemies operate on fixed values and unique behaviours, ignoring player Prism constraints. This allows the GM to create "impossible" threats that require specific card(s) or behaviour as answers. An enemy's "Statblock" is essentially their Intent Loop/Algorithm + their HP. Ex.: Husk = 7 HP Intent: Strike (6) -> Guard (8) -> Strike (8) -> Guard (12) -> LOOP. This makes enemies predictable (rewards knowledge/experience and learning), and decreases GM's mental load drastically, while keeping the players on their toes whenever they meet something new. Enemy GUARD does* stack innately. Enemies are tracked by laying out their individual cards in a 5-wide and 3-tall grid.*
The Frontline is composed of the first two rows, while the third row is reserved for REINFORCEMENTS. Some enemy Loops may change depending on their position. Potential enemy-tracker card. Has space for Name, two diamonds for HP and Guard, big box for an illustration, and a semicircle meant to house their intent.*

item.title: REINFORCEMENTS
item.description: The Topmost row of enemies are the Reinforcements. These enemies cannot yet act, and cannot be targeted. As soon as an enemy in front of them perishes, the Reinforcement from the same column drops down, as if affected by gravity. Enemies may be added into the Reinforcements row as part of enemy LOOPS or as part of the encounter itself.

item.title: LOOPS
item.description:
BOXES: Red - STRIKE (aimed at the Hearth)
Blue - GUARD (Usually on itself, but full HP enemies can guard Damaged enemies) Yellow - Conditions (Placed alongside the original Intent) Can also mean Debuffs.
Grey/Green - STATUS Cards (shuffled into the shared deck immediately) LINES symbolise adapting patterns which change the next intent (after the one currently shown): Red - If hit Blue - if took no DMG last turn. Grey: standard progression **Encounter Templates Use composition, not scaling. Skirmish (~60%) – quick and risky (death favours those who wait). Annoyance (~20%) – awkward pressure (backline pests). Attrition (~15%) – force reshuffles. Wildcard/Boss (~5%) – break the rules. GM rule of thumb: Party handles ~N+1 enemy actions per round comfortably.

item.title: MIGHT
item.description: Physical force, momentum, and brute-force attrition. Embers specialising in MIGHT are usually assertive, problem-oriented, and back down only when struck down. They bring a sense of power and pride into the Hearth, often keeping the morale up by sheer presence alone. Keywords: STRIKE, MOMENTUM, BLEED

item.title: INSIGHT
item.description: Tactical manipulation, force multiplication, and metaphysical stability. Embers specialising in INSIGHT are usually the strangest of the three, distant and collected, they bring this stability and calm into the Hearth, as they balance the other Oaths' strengths and weaknesses, the only flaw they possess is dependency. Keywords: EMPOWER, IGNITE, MEND.

item.title: GUILE
item.description: Precision, resource management, and reactive defence. Embers specialising in GUILE think on their feet, under pressure and under fire. They're the ones who pop their heads up first when something occurs, but they will seldom use force, instead relying on their instinct, and their Hearth. They add a sense of competence into the bond, a feeling that any problem can be solved if everyone just thinks for a second. Keywords: GUARD, BRACE, COUNTER

item.title: CHARGE
item.description:
Each player has an individual Ember Gauge. Building up: Play: +1 Charge per 1 ENERGY spent on your cards.
Sacrifice: +2 Charges per 1 ENERGY cost of a card willingly discarded from hand. You still must pay the card's cost to discard it, but it has no effect unless described otherwise. Charges can stack up indefinitely. Activation:
Costs 10 Charges. Every subsequent activation during one EXPLORATION costs +5 Charges for that particular Ember's Charge. After Charging, the Hearth draws 1 Card less next turn. Subsequent Charges (even from the same Character) in the same turn, add a stacking +1 to this penalty. (*So a second Charge makes the penalty 1 + 1+1. Then the third increases it to 1 + 1+1 + 1+1+1, etc.) Additional Effects: Every 10 Charges stored, gain +1 to the values of ALL your scalable Cards. However, every time you increase this bonus, roll 1d100. Rolling a number lower than your current Charges causes you to take 1d6 DMG for every 10 Charges held.

item.title: OATH_OF_THE_PYRE
item.description:
Your Prism scores are as follows: 2 MIGHT
1 GUILE
0 INSIGHT You may allocate a single point to a chosen Prism. Your Starting deck consists of: Either;
6x STRIKE
3x GUARD Or;
6x GUARD
3x STRIKE
And, 1x MOMENTUM

item.title: CHARGES
item.description:
Sacrifice up to MIGHT VITALS, gain as much BRACE, and 2 X MOMENTUM
Gain GUARD = MIGHT X N (Player Count)
STRIKE with MOMENTUM multiplier (DMG = STRIKE X MOMENTUM, instead of STRIKE + MOMENTUM).
MEND Half Max VITALS, grant MIGHT CHARGE charges to another Ember.
STRIKE ALL Front enemies, BLEED ALL Back enemies.
Pause up to MIGHT enemy Intents. Lose 3 ENERGY for every Intent Paused this way.

item.title: OATH_OF_THE_MISTWALKER
item.description:
Your Prism scores are as follows: 1 MIGHT
2 GUILE
0 INSIGHT You may allocate a single point to a chosen Prism. Your Starting deck consists of:
3x STRIKE
5x GUARD
2x COUNTER

item.title: MISTWALKER PASSIVES
item.description:
Dealing unblocked HP damage grants +1 CHARGE.
Discard the first negative STATUS drawn (by You) each turn without triggering its effects.
Start each combat with GUILE BRACE and MOMENTUM.
Landing a final blow on an enemy lets you DRAW 1 Card OR regain 1 ENERGY.
At the Start of each of your turns, gain +1 BRACE.
First COUNTER played each turn reduces a chosen enemy's GUARD by the same value.

item.title: CHARGES
item.description:
Reduce the cost of all cards currently dealt to 1 this turn. Next card played this turn takes effect 2 extra times (so 3 activations total). Stun an enemy. (Pause their Intent) Draw GUILE cards, regain 1 ENERGY, EXHAUST all STATUS cards currently dealt.
ALL played COUNTER also acts as STRIKE with equal Value this turn.
STRIKE = 2 DMG. Multiplied by 2x for every STATUS currently dealt.

item.title: OATH_OF_THE_CONDUIT
item.description:
Your Prism scores are as follows: 0 MIGHT
1 GUILE
2 INSIGHT You may allocate a single point to a chosen Prism. Your Starting deck consists of:
3x STRIKE
3x GUARD
2x EMPOWER
2x MEND

item.title: CHARGES
item.description:
Enemy loses all GUARD and COUNTER stacks.
STRIKE = Missing VITALS
Play up to INSIGHT cards for free, if they scale with INSIGHT (from other players too).
IGNITE INSIGHT Distribute the stacks as you wish. Already IGNITEd enemies take their total IGNITE stacks as DMG immediately (Does not remove stacks).
Add current BRACE and MOMENTUM together to one of the two, then gain INSIGHT of the other. Push/Pull an enemy to a different Row, Advance their Intent (skip the current one in favour of the next).

item.title: TOOLS
item.description:
UTILITIES Light Sources - provide vision in dark areas as long as they're burning. Torch Lantern Rations - necessary to regain Vitals during a Rest. Compass - inquiry about location. Will / Soul Compass Map - inquiry about location. Watch - inquiry about the time. Binoculars - for viewing things at a distance. Radio - for saying things at a distance. Spare Clothes - social decency. Soap / other Grooming supplies - social decency. Chalk - marking. Divining Bones - rituals, superstitions. Oath Tokens - given when first incorporated into a Hearth ADVANTAGES Lockpicks - Reroll up to Half your rolled dice when attempting to open a lock. Crowbar - +2 Successes when attempting to open something that's locked. Glass cutter - Add +1 to all your rolled faces when cutting glass. Medical Kit - Remove 1 card added to the deck via the Trauma rule. Needs a Rest, uses up the Medical Kit. Stimulants - Improve one's reflexes, perception, or capabilities for one check. Coffee +3 Dice rolled on INSIGHT checks.
Painkillers +3 Dice rolled on MIGHT checks.
Heroin +3 Dice rolled on GUILE checks.
Bond-Blood Transfusion - restore Half of the Hearth's VITALS. Can only take one between Rests. Forged Documents - Useful when trying to pass off as someone else. Bribe Money - Useful when dealing with officials.
Accelerant - Consume to gain 3 CHARGE charges. GATEKEEPS Rope / Grappling hook - Allow climbing walls, cliffs, and crossing chasms. Hammer & Spikes - Allow rapid architecture. (Boarding up a door / window, fixing a hole) Vehicle Repair Kit - Allow tinkering with, repairing, and stealing vehicles.
Face Wrap / Mask - Disable the Madness mechanic when traversing the MIST. Official Permits - Allow access to restricted areas. Fuel - Allows running vehicles, generators, and machinery. Breaching Charge - Open most basic doors instantly, allow attempting to open heavy / secure doors.

item.title: ARTEFACTS
item.description:
These are rare, non-consumable items that sit on the Hearth Sheet (Alongside VITALS, ENERGY, etc.). They provide non-Oath Passive Effects to the Hearth, but have either low number values, or specific conditions to make them click. Examples would Include:
DRAW +1 card whenever you draw a STATUS card, BUT Enemy Yellow intents gain +1 potency.
MEND is Doubled when at Max/Min SYNC-AXIS, BUT -1 ENERGY Max.
Adds PIERCE to the first STRIKE each turn, BUT Adds 1x Husk.canvas to every combat.
When playing a STRIKE and a GUARD consecutively add SYNC-AXIS score to both. (Negative values are in fact negative) The Final card played each turn gains +1 to its highest value keyword for every card played before it this turn, BUT the first card played every turn gets -1 to its highest value keyword.
Not playing a GUARD grants +2 DRAW next turn, BUT Incoming DMG +1.
Not playing a STRIKE grants +2 ENERGY next turn, BUT Adds 1x Witch.canvas to every combat.
Killing an enemy Draws N cards, BUT reshuffling the deck adds 2xN STATUS cards now.
MEND also EXHAUST a selected STATUS card currently held, BUT Card removal during LEVEL_UP costs Double.
Reduce CHARGE extra cost (from repeated use) by 5 after a LEVEL_UP, BUT MEND can only be played at <50% VITALS. Cannot reduce CHARGE cost below the base cost. See the incoming enemy Intents, BUT +1 enemy intent realised every round.
Non-Core BACKBONE cards gain +3 Value, BUT Hand size -1. The Hearth can carry 3 Artifacts at once.

item.title: CURIOS
item.description:
A Curio’s effect should be noticeable once every 2–3 fights, and never decisive. Curios can: modify a specific card tweak a keyword bend a rule slightly Curios can and should: Compete with each other (Max of 1 Curio link per Keyword. Does not apply for Curios that add Keywords) Overwrite (Freshest applied Curio overrules) If the card or the Curio is ever removed, the effect is lost. Grants One of your STRIKE cards VAMPIRIC.
Grants One of your GUARD cards +1 to its Value.
Counts as Two SHARDS (can be recycled). Changes which Prism a specific Keyword on a single Card scales with.
Makes a card take effect Twice, then EXHAUST. Halve a card's cost and all its numerical effects. Give +1 charge when Discarding a card its linked to.

item.title: INVENTORY
item.description:
Inventory is strictly divided to separate narrative utility from mechanical progression. Tools (3 Slots): Functional items TOOLS. These grant Advantage (+Success) on specific BS ROLLS (CHECKS), or remove Gatekeeping. Gatekeeping: Certain tasks (climbing a sheer cliff) are impossible (DC ) without the specific item (Rope).
Curios (3 Slots): Personal narrative items or unique trophies. CURIOS can influence one card/one keyword on one card at a time. Shards (Numerical Value): Weightless essence stored directly in the Ember Deck. Coin Pouch: Traditional currency used for mundane trade (food, lodging, information).
Armaments (Swords, Staves, Plate) are Narrative Justifications. They do not occupy slots because they are the physical justification of your 10-card deck. A player doesn't "get a sword" for +1 DMG; they have STRIKE cards because they have means to deal that DMG (like a pistol, sabre, or dedicated fist).

item.title: CUSTOMS
item.description:
Custom Cards have more than One Keyword. Can be UNIQUE or upgraded from BACKBONE cards.
Card ENERGY Cost Scales with Keyword count (1:1), making complex cards more expensive to play.
They use only the related Prism's Value for any scaling effects, where applicable, without the BACKBONE Reliability Bonus.

item.title: BACKBONE_DECAY
item.description: Backbones lose 1 point of their bonus per Rest or Level-Up. Oaths of permanence: at character creation, the player chooses four cards that are locked in as their signature core, these Backbone cards will never Decay through rests, but can also never be removed or altered via SHARDS. ARTEFACTS and CURIOS still apply. That gives the Players: a stable identity, a way to keep some Backbone value permanently, Any other Backbone cards can still decay, evolve, or be replaced over time.

item.title: BACKBONE
item.description: Starter Cards with only One Keyword, they have an innate +3 Value. Backbone cards are limited to the single-keyword cards provided in the Starting Oath Deck. Adding any keyword or modifying them turns them into Custom cards, removing the +3 bonus. Likewise, removing extra Keywords from a CUSTOMS card brings it back to a Backbone once its left with just One Keyword. Deck Rule: A player’s deck must always contain at least 4 Backbone Cards, as detailed below.

item.title: TALK_THE_TALK
item.description: Affinity (1-10) is the long-term relationship state, hidden from players and used as the underlying modifier. Affinity can be earned or lost depending on player actions and/or reputation. Each of the examples given can also be reversed to achieve the opposite effect. It usually moves in increments of 1-2 points at a time, dependent on campaign length. Positive Actions: Fulfilling an NPC's request (ex. Doing the job you're hired for) Siding with them in an argument (ex. Helping the barman rid of an out of control drunk) Doing something they benefit from (ex. Shutting down a local mafioso that was threatening them - Important! The Players do not need to know how and who they're benefitting when taking an action to still be viewed positively) Negative Actions: Siding with the opposite side of the argument (ex. Joining the aforementioned drunkard) Stealing from or harming the NPC directly (ex. Injuring the barman when a fight erupts between patrons) Doing something that affects them negatively (ex. letting a cult blow up their house by being late to the scene - Important! The NPC has to know about something to be upset over it) Patience is a derived scene clock: Affinity × an NPC/situation-specific multiplier (1-5). This multiplier is entirely in the hands of the GM, but as a rough reference guide: 1–2: distracted, busy, hostile environment 3: normal conversation 4–5: relaxed, safe, or invested Players do not see the scores themselves. They only get narrative clues, shifts in tone, body language, and response quality. Insight can dampen Patience damage, so socially skilled characters can prolong or soften conversations. When Patience runs out, the NPC ends the exchange, refuses further pressure, or forces the scene to close. Base Patience damage per investigative question/request: 6 Minor provocation / repetitive pressure: 9 Major push / insult / contradiction: 12 Insight dampens this DMG by its raw value, but cannot reduce it below 1. Once per exchange, when the players introduce new, relevant leverage / a meaningful concession / impactful information, the tone of the conversation may shift. If the leverage is significant, Patience loss from the next interaction is reduced (bring the DMG down one level 12->9->6->1), and the NPC’s response changes accordingly. Repeated or low-value attempts have no effect, or may even insult the NPC. A pivot must do at least one of these: Change the stakes (“If you don’t help, this gets worse for you too”) Reveal something relevant (not trivia, actual leverage) Offer real cost (risk, debt, obligation—not pocket change) Reframe the situation (make the NPC reconsider their position) When Patience would reach 0, the players may attempt one final appeal: [offer leverage / make a meaningful concession]. If the offer is compelling, the NPC allows one additional exchange and may adjust Affinity [positively / negatively]. If it fails, the conversation ends immediately and Affinity is reduced. The offer must be one of: Value (money, item, favour) Information (useful, relevant, new) Commitment (promise with cost or risk) If it’s not one of those, it’s just stalling. NPC's can also receive Quirks if they're relevant enough or just appear often in a game or its segment. These are additional modifiers to the rules above so use them sparingly. As a Player, feel free to add nuance to your character by borrowing some of these, maybe even in combination!

item.title: LEVEL_UP
item.description:
Level-Ups occur on NODE - REST/between EXPLORATIONs, and use SHARDS accumulated during travels, which allow for: Increasing a single Prism's Value (-5 shards)
Removing Cards (-3 shards) *A player’s deck must always contain at least 4 BACKBONE Cards from their Core selection.
Adding Keywords to Cards (-3 shards for Primary, -2 shards for Secondary and Tertiary, -1 for CURSED) When adding a Keyword to a card, you may (only) choose a Keyword that scales with a Prism you have at least 1 point in.
Purging permanent STATUS cards (-3 shards)
Adding new BACKBONE Cards (-2 shards) *When adding a new Card, it is encouraged to follow the New Keyword rule from above, unless you are planning several steps ahead of course. Changing Keywords already on Cards (-2)
Restoring EXHAUSTed cards (-1) Removing Keywords from Cards (+1)

item.title: BS ROLLS (CHECKS)
item.description: Used to resolve arguments or environmental obstacles. Pool: 2d6 Base + Prism X d6. Success: Each die showing 4, 5, or 6. Sixes count as double. Failure: Each die showing 3, 2, or 1. Difficulty: DC 1-2: Standard Task. DC 3: Strenuous. DC 4: Dangerous/Elite. DC 6+: Near-impossible. Extra Difficulty: Roll 2x of the same Value (ex. 2x3) - Precise Task Roll 3x of the same Value - Surgical Task Roll 4x of the same Value - Stars must Align...

item.title: NODE - UNSTABLE
item.description: A node that creates a one-way path once crossed, cannot be crossed again.

item.title: NODE - UNKNOWN
item.description:
Its in the name. If no light source or hazmat equipment is in use, Add 1x MADNESS Contains: 0-50 - Enemy Encounter 51-80 - Empty 81-90 - A Single Shard
91-100 - A single STATUS from this selection (1d6):
WOUND
PANIC
DOUBT
INJURY
TREMBLING
MIST

item.title: NODE - RITUAL
item.description: Gain N shards, but take a random status into the deck. Gain access to a rare Curio or Artefact, but lose time / trigger a hostile encounter. Gain Affinity with a Society/NPC, but offend another one.

item.title: NODE - REWARD
item.description: A tile containing either a Key (used to unlock NODE - DOOR), TOOLS, or CURIOS, or even ARTEFACTS.

item.title: NODE - REST
item.description: A space designated for LEVEL_UP and rejuvenation. Reusable.

item.title: NODE - PORTAL
item.description: Stepping on either of the two linked tiles transports you immediately to the other one.

item.title: NODE - PATH
item.description: An empty hex, allows free travel at a steady pace.

item.title: NODE - ENCOUNTER
item.description: A node hosting a combat or social encounter, marked appropriately depending on the hostility of the situation and the threat level.

item.title: NODE - DOOR
item.description: A tile that is Blocked until a matching Key is found to unlock it.

item.title: EXPLORATION
item.description: Exploration is done on a Hex-grid, where the contents of specific Hexes are revealed only a short distance away, but the path as a whole can be viewed instantly (Boardgame reality) It is possible to split the party but doing so usually harbours more risk than reward. To move, every player rolls a D6, then, the rolled sides are added together, and divided by N. Basically: Every time your rolls add up to N, get +1 Movement, and keep adding any rolled dice left. You can then move as far as your Movement allows on the map, and repeat the process.

item.title: TRENCH-DOCTRINES
item.description:
The first time each combat the Hearth would take DMG, reduce it by N.The first successful Threat each scene restores N Patience.At combat start, draw N additional cards.
Enemies entering Reinforcements immediately take N DMG.12 Swap two adjacent map nodes.8+
Gain N Brace.4+ Learn an NPC's Quirk.10+ Delay an enemy's intent.Reduce DMG taken by 1. Reduce DMG dealt by 1.6+
Gain N Guard. Fail: take 2 DMG.8+
NODE - PREDATOR moves in the opposite direction this turn. Fail:
NODE - PREDATOR moves twice.Movement DC increased by 1 (Now, N+1).

item.title: NOMAD-DOCTRINES
item.description:
6+ Reroll movement.10+ NODE - PREDATOR stops moving for this turn.Movement DC reduced by 1.
The first NODE - UNSTABLE crossed each exploration does not collapse.
NODE - PREDATOR move every other turn instead of every turn.4+ Move 1 extra hex this movement phase.4+
Target backline enemy (one-time Pierce).10+
FLEEING the current combat without the usual check.10+ Ignore a Node’s negative effect when entering it. Fail: movement phase immediately ends.
Reveal adjacent NODE - UNKNOWN before entering them.
NODE - PREDATOR moves immediately when revealed.6+ Reveal chosen enemy Encounter. Fail:
chosen encounter becomes a NODE - PREDATOR .
If already a NODE - PREDATOR , it moves once.Every reshuffle deals 1 DMG to the Hearth.

item.title: MAGIC-DOCTRINES
item.description:
12+ All players gain Charge equal to their highest Prism.
At the start of combat, every player gains 1 Charge.
When a player gains Charge, all other players gain 1 Charge.
First Charge gained each combat is doubled.
First Charge check each exploration gains +10 to the roll.
Every 10 Charges held grants +2 keyword values instead of +1.
Retain a card each turn.4+
Draw N.6+
Gain 1 Charge each. Fail:
immediate Charge check.10+
Transfer N Charge between players. Fail:
Gain N Ignite.
All players gain 2 Charge at combat start.
Charge failures roll an additional d6 DMG.Charge failures roll an additional d6 DMG.

item.title: HEROIC-DOCTRINES
item.description:
10+ Ignore a reduction to 0 Vitals.
First Status shuffled into the deck each combat is ignored.
Breaking Guard grants 1 Charge.First movement roll each exploration gains +1.Once per combat, double a card’s final value.10+
Dying enemies won't trigger Reinforcements this turn.8+
Gain N momentum.2+
Gain +1 keyword value. You may no longer FLEEING from this encounter.Successful Threats restore N Patience. Requests always deal at least 6 Patience DMG.8+ Double a played card's value. Fail:
discard that card instead.8+
All allies gain N Charge. Fail:
enemies gain N Guard.
Fleeing from encounters becomes impossible.

item.title: DIESEL-DOCTRINES
item.description:
12 Gain 1 SHARDS.
After defeating an enemy, every player gains 1 Charge.
Whenever a player Exhaust a card, draw 1.
After the first reshuffle each combat draw +1 card.
The first exhaust card each combat returns to hand next turn.8+ Increase next keyword's value by +N.
Gain N Guard every turn while below half Vitals.4+
Strike X.The first card each player uses every combat gains +1 to its keyword value.
discard ALL cards at turn end.6+
Immediately reshuffle the discard pile into the draw pile.4+
Draw 2. Discard 1.Enemies never flee.

item.title: A Guide to Doctrines
item.description: At doctrine selection: choose 1 of 3 normally. OR Take a Burden Doctrine, which is an immediate downside, but, gain access to another doctrine choice (1 of 3) afterward. Some Doctrines are purely Passive effects, but others require a Check. When trying to activate a Doctrine, roll 2d6, and compare the roll's results to the DC. These Doctrine Checks can only be attempted once per scene A Scene is a single combat encounter, an exploration, or a Social encounter. It is worth differentiating that while combats and social interactions happen with the grand exploration map in the background, every time the players return to the hex grid it is treated as a new scene for this purpose. Basic, simple effects.Reliable and Moderate.Specialized.Powerful doctrines only.A one-in-a-session kind of effect. when making a character, (maybe in "advanced character creation", akin to the Quirks table) agree on one of the Societies. Your Hearth starts with +1 Allegiance with that society. to get a given society's cards into the pool you need at least 1 allegiance with them. At 3+ allegiance you get access to more rare and powerful doctrines from that society. Societies with <1 allegiance will have only their Burdens/Gambits doctrines in the main pool. Where your HQ/Hideout/Base of operations is, based on that settlements society, gain +1 allegiance with them. Works better in campaigns than one shots, as it allows the players to actually pick themselves. taking assignments from a given society gives +1 allegiance, fulfilling said assignment gives another +1, BUT failing or dropping the assignment will give a -2 (lose the initial +1 given and then lose 1 more) Important: since Gambit choices are available essentially always for every Society, they should be the most flavourful. A group not allegied with, say, Trench society should be able to get a Risky offer from them and think "hey these guys have a neat play style, let's engage with them more after this". ONLY through: Character origin Establishing HQ Accepting long-term assignments Completing major operations Formal alliances ONLY through: Betrayal Failed assignments Public opposition Supporting rivals Abandonment

item.title: VIBE
item.description: Ember Deck is a dieselpunk low‑fantasy setting: think late‑1920s/1930s industry, artillery, engines, and trench maps, but soaked in ash, bad weather, and magical fallout. Magic is everywhere, yet almost nobody uses it well; most people can maybe spark a torch or vent a small surge, while precise, repeatable spellwork is rare and risky. The default tone is dark and gritty: the world is miserable to live in, people assume the worst, and survival choices often matter more than ideals. The Great War never truly stopped; it shattered into endless local wars over resources and defensible ground. Borders, regimes, and even school maps keep changing every few years as frontlines creep back and forth, so people trust their city, their unit, or their convoy far more than any abstract “nation.” Travel beyond a well‑lit Hearth is frightening even for trained Embers because the GREY MIST, Hollows, and roaming armies make the world outside feel half‑lost and actively hostile.In‑world, people talk about “magic,” but underneath it behaves more like fallout from an overtaxed soul‑furnace than like a clean spell system. The first world‑scale war came early, killed staggering numbers of people, and overloaded Hell, which in this setting is a processing furnace for souls rather than a moral destination. When the furnace could not keep up, the overflow bled back into the world as raw, dangerous soul‑energy; that leak is what people now experience as magic. Because of that origin, magic behaves more like radiation or pressure than like a tame tool. It builds up in living bodies, causing telltale signs like glowing veins and eyes, fever, and cracked skin, and if it is not safely vented or shared through a Hearth, it can trigger collapse into a Hollow. Anyone can coax a small effect into existence, but pushing for more power without training, support, and cooling is like stepping into a reactor core: the strength is there, but precision and survival are not.The GREY MIST is the other half of the disaster: it is the cold, impersonal “will” of all that unprocessed soul‑stuff leaking into reality. It does not carry feelings, only fragments of memory and belief, and it clings to low‑light, low‑heat areas; that is why cities burn lanterns even at noon and why darkness is treated as a safety hazard, not an aesthetic choice. Walking into thick Mist can mean hallucinations, personality bleed, and temporary loss of self as your mind is pressed against countless other impressions. When a person’s internal load of soul‑energy spikes too far, they do not explode outward; instead, their “soul container” implodes and fails. That process strips away memory and identity into the Mist and leaves behind a Hollow: a thermally vitrified, zombie‑like shell that shambles toward heat and life because residual soul‑heat seeks equilibrium. Hollows are exterminated on sight not because they are evil, but because everyone knows they were people yesterday and that collapse can happen to anyone whose system fails.Player characters are Embers, people whose spark flared bright enough that they can work significant magic but at the cost of burning through their own lifespan. To survive, most Embers are bound into Hearths: tightly linked groups whose soul‑bond spreads the heat and pressure across multiple people, allowing safer venting, shared emotional regulation, and somewhat cleaner channelling of power. The bond shapes both personality and combat style, and mechanically it affects shared energy, damage mitigation, and how well the group fights together.When a Hearth bond forms, it may be due to ritual, traumatic shared experience, or long, intense closeness; over time, members often lose or blur their memories of who they were before, which weakens traditional ideas of a fixed “self.” If a bond breaks catastrophically and an Ember survives, they become a Cinder: a lone, unstable caster with power but no damping, prone to predation on others or complete mental break. Cinders make excellent antagonists because they know they are burning out and often respond by consuming captives as fuel or lashing out at the world that failed them.States, cities, and factions treat Hearths, Embers, and even Cinders as weapons first and everything else second. Military service is universal but intentionally short; strategists learned that extended deployment makes people Hollow out faster, so they rotate bodies through the grinder to extract usefulness before collapse. With the Great War fragmented into local conflicts, each resource‑rich or defensible location becomes a prize, and everyone expects conscription, propaganda, and some level of normalized atrocity as part of everyday life. For players, this means the default expectation is not clean heroism. Characters are allowed—often encouraged—to be compromised: noble in some ways and selfish in others, more concerned with keeping their Hearth alive, securing a working rail line, or preventing a Cinder from devouring a village than with “saving the world.” The setting supports investigative missions, deniable operations, front‑line actions, and dirty survival work where magic feels potent but dangerous, the world pushes back hard, and every victory leaves scars.

item.title: Scrap of Paper Found Inside a Helmet
item.description: I think the worst part is that they still run like people.If they ran like animals maybe it would be easier.But they don’t.They stumble.They brace themselves on walls.Sometimes they protect their heads from falling debris.Like they remember being alive just enough to make this cruel.
item.guid: LORE/In-World/Personal/Scrap of Paper Found Inside a Helmet.md

item.title: Diary Fragment
item.description: 1st March, 1933We caught some sunlight today.Real sunlight.Not shellfire.Not signal flares.Just sunlight through the fog for maybe three, four seconds.Nobody spoke while it happened.Even the artillery seemed to quiet down for once.I think we all forgot it still existed.

item.title: HIGH PRIORITY FRONTLINE DISPATCH
item.description: To all active line officers stationed across the Northern Front:Reconnaissance confirms enemy reinforcement consolidation across sectors D-11 through H-2. Continued passive engagement will result in artillery enclosure within an estimated ██ days.As of 03:40 this morning, High Command has authorized Operation: GLASS LANTERN.All forward divisions are hereby ordered to initiate aggressive night advancement procedures against entrenched hostile positions.Gain sufficient ground to establish forward raiding corridors for continued nocturnal disruption campaigns.Force enemy rotation instability through sustained trench penetration.Projected casualty expectations remain within acceptable wartime thresholds.Officers are reminded: hesitation under current conditions constitutes strategic sabotage.Mist density across no-man’s-land is expected to reach hazardous levels during the operation window. Ember detachments will therefore advance alongside standard infantry waves despite previous sustainability concerns.Rotations exceeding standard Collapse exposure thresholds have been temporarily authorized.Medical personnel are instructed to: suppress panic responses, maintain stimulant distribution, report signs of pre-Collapse behaviour immediately. Under no circumstances are retreat orders to be issued without direct authorization from Northern Command.The line must move forward.Even a kilometre.Especially a kilometre.Signed, Marshal ████████ Northern Command Authority

item.title: INTERNAL INCIDENT REPORT
item.description: At approximately 14:17 local time, officers stationed near the eastern produce lanes observed signs of an imminent Collapse Event within civilian crowds.Subject identified as: ████████████████ Male Approximate age: 34-41 Employment records pending retrieval.Witnesses reported: visible tremors, facial distress, uncontrolled thermal leakage from ocular cavities and fingertips. Officer Halvern attempted standard containment procedure and issued evacuation orders approximately seven seconds before ignition. The subject entered active Collapse state before suppression measures could be deployed.Effects included: immediate thermal detonation, localized ignition spread, secondary panic surge, trampling casualties, infrastructural fire damage. Total confirmed fatalities: ██ Missing: ████ Unrecoverable remains: ██████████Of note: Several witnesses reported hearing the subject speaking prior to ignition. Statements vary heavily and are considered unreliable due to post-event distress.No evidence currently supports the existence of organized hostile involvement.Official public explanation issued: Industrial chemical detonation caused by illegal heating equipment. This explanation is to remain consistent across all press channels.Any mention of; Collapse Events, spontaneous ignition, Husk manifestation, resonance instability, is classified under Emergency Silence Accord ██-C. Further dissemination punishable by military tribunal.

item.title: Trench Journal of Senior Fortification Engineer Armin Vale
item.description: Northern Front, Sector C-9Entry 218Six hours today.Maybe seven.Hard to tell anymore with the fog.The spotters rotate every two. Builders every four. Engineers stay until relieved because apparently we are less likely to “drift cognitively.” That is the phrase Command uses now. Drift cognitively...The Mist was calm today.That's bad.When it moves, at least you can track it. The drifting gives the eyes something to measure. But when it sits still beyond the wire you begin imagining shapes where there are none. Or worse.We lost another spotter last night.No screaming. No nothing. He just climbed over the trench wall during supper count and walked into the fog. Rifle still strapped over his shoulder like he was heading for patrol.Nobody followed him.Protocol says nobody follows them anymore.Corporal Mern says he saw someone standing in the Mist watching us afterward. Not a Husk. Too tall. Didn’t move right.Mern also hasn’t slept properly in eleven days, so that's something.Deployment ends next week.At least that is what they told us three weeks ago.The Mist just sits there, as do I.And every day it feels a little closer than yesterday.
item.guid: LORE/In-World/Personal/Trench Journal of Senior Fortification Engineer Armin Vale.md

item.title: Recovered Journal Page
item.description: 12th July, 1941I did not stay to see the bodies.It sounds cowardly, I know, but there are things the body decides on before the mind has a chance to speak, and this sure was one of them. One moment I'm standing there listening to a butcher argue over salt prices, and the next everyone was screaming like animals.Not all at once either.First came the sound.Like steam escaping a pipe.Then the smell.The man, I think it was a man, grabbed his own face before it happened. I remember that clearly because at first I thought he was laughing. His shoulders were shaking and people nearby stepped away thinking he was drunk.Then the light came through his fingers.Not around them.Through them.Orange first. Then white.I remember seeing the bones in his hands like candles inside paper lanterns.Someone shouted “COLLAPSE” and that was enough. The crowd folded in on itself immediately. People started climbing over market stalls before the blast even happened. Funny thing is I don’t think the explosion killed most of them. Panic did. Trampled each other without a second thought.The sound after the ignition is difficult to describe. Not loud. I expected loud. It was more like the air itself tearing open.I saw a woman’s coat catch fire without touching the flames.That was when I ran.I know what the papers will say tomorrow. Sudden ignition. Isolated instability event. Citizens are reminded to report signs of emotional agitation. Same old phrases.But I saw his eyes before it happened.There was no panic in them.Only exhaustion.And I think that frightens me more than the fire.

item.title: GREY MIST
item.description:
While EROSION comes from the unprocessed, wandering Souls, the Mist is the Will. It doesn't carry emotions, its suffocatingly cold, and imbued with memories, beliefs. Venturing into it is almost guaranteed to cause hallucinations, temporary personality disorders, and psychosis from mind-homogenisation. The leak is global. Gravity, wind, and geography don’t matter much when the source is outside reality. Low light allows it to accumulate. Heat and light disperse it. Cities burn lanterns even at noon. Darkness isn’t romantic, but hazardous.


“ce85dfe435a1b3b7dd6108af4a66809d.jpg” could not be found.

item.title: CRS
item.description: Government-dependent bodies that handle the population's EROSION. They set up facilities of cold stone and water to allow for safe venting, as well as taking in the "terminal" cases, which they handle out back. They wear layered uniforms, like toxic hazard workers. High-status, low-life-expectancy, trained to read early collapse signs. People accept: Lantern taxes Data indexing Witchhunt-like inspections Not because they trust authority, but because they’ve seen what happens when someone collapses in a crowd.

item.title: a7ec12281440fa8cdecd6a8734c7ed2b
a97f0bfdd87199f0c657c672d26255c0
COLLAPSE
item.description: Stage 1: "The Burnups" Chronic fever, ash coughing Veins, eyes, fingernails, hair ends light up Heightened Emotional states Fire responds to your presence Stage 2: Burst One moment too much: A stored surge A panicked release A battlefield spike The affected spontaneously combusts, and as the fire dies down, Memory, identity, intent - gone, into the GREY MIST. Stage 3: Hollowing (fractions of a second after Breach) Organs char Blood thickens into slag Nerves fire randomly from thermal stress The heart becomes a smouldering knot Skin cracks like fired clay The person is already gone. Stage 4: The Husk Walks A Husk: Avoids nothing Moves toward thermal gradients like crowds, fires, battlefields Why? Because residual soul-heat seeks other heat to equalize with. Husks drift toward the living, or at the very least towards where they reside. That’s why they’re exterminated on sight.

item.title: c86ff00fd698870aa3acbb579a94d3bb
0102b319a571517da5b1b645b64586c5
8c2cbaa744928c161d8d7356cc1aea87
